req.title: Hr-Admin views timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-020 requires an authenticated hr-admin to view a timesheet via UI, with mandatory field validation and audit logging. The test suite covers the happy path (positive), authentication/authorization failures (negative), and boundary conditions on payload size and field constraints (boundary). Tests are designed using Equivalence Partitioning (valid/invalid auth states), Boundary Value Analysis (max field lengths), Decision Table (role/auth combinations), and Negative Path (missing auth, forbidden roles). All tests are self-contained, deterministic, and idempotent; they mock or use test fixtures to avoid external dependencies.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_views_timesheet_success
preconditions: hr-admin is authenticated with valid JWT token
timesheet with ID 'ts-001' exists in database
actions: Assert response contains entity id and timesheet data
Verify audit record created in audit log table
expected: Audit table contains entry: action='VIEW', resource='timesheet', resource_id='ts-001', actor='hr-admin'
trace: R-WEB-020: authenticated hr-admin shall be able to view timesheet
Expected outcomes: timesheet is viewed; audit record written; response 200 or 201
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest
from datetime import datetime

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('auth_token', 'db_session')
def test_hr_admin_views_timesheet_success(auth_token, db_session, api_url):
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    response = requests.get(f'{api_url}/timesheets/ts-001', headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert 'id' in data and data['id'] == 'ts-001'
    assert 'employee_id' in data
    assert 'total_hours' in data
    audit_record = db_session.query(AuditLog).filter_by(
        action='VIEW', resource_id='ts-001'
    ).order_by(AuditLog.created_at.desc()).first()
    assert audit_record is not None
    assert audit_record.actor == 'hr-admin'

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet
expected: No audit log entry for this request
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('db_session')
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_timesheet(db_session, api_url):
    response = requests.get(f'{api_url}/timesheets/ts-001')
    assert response.status_code == 401
    assert 'unauthorized' in response.text.lower() or 'authentication' in response.text.lower()
    audit_record = db_session.query(AuditLog).filter_by(
        action='VIEW', resource_id='ts-001'
    ).order_by(AuditLog.created_at.desc()).first()
    assert audit_record is None

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_role_cannot_view_timesheet
preconditions: user is authenticated with valid JWT token
actions: GET /timesheets/ts-001 with valid token but employee role
expected: No audit log entry created for view action
trace: R-WEB-020: Error pathways: 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_view_timesheet_with_max_length_id
preconditions: timesheet ID is at maximum allowed length (255 chars)
expected: Timesheet data returned with correct ID
Audit record created with full ID value
trace: R-WEB-020: boundary condition on entity identifier length
tests.executable:
import requests
import pytest

@pytest.mark.usefixtures('auth_token', 'db_session')
def test_view_timesheet_with_max_length_id(auth_token, db_session, api_url):
    max_id = 'ts-' + 'x' * 252
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {auth_token}'}
    response = requests.get(f'{api_url}/timesheets/{max_id}', headers=headers)
    assert response.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = response.json()
    assert data['id'] == max_id
    audit_record = db_session.query(AuditLog).filter_by(
        resource_id=max_id
    ).order_by(AuditLog.created_at.desc()).first()
    assert audit_record is not None

tests.name: test_view_nonexistent_timesheet_returns_404
preconditions: timesheet with ID 'ts-nonexistent' does not exist
actions: Verify no audit record created for nonexistent resource
expected: No audit log entry for this request
trace: R-WEB-020: error handling for missing resource

tests.name: test_view_timesheet_with_malformed_token
preconditions: Authorization header contains malformed JWT token
trace: R-WEB-020: error pathways: 401 on authentication failure (malformed token)
